title: Pi-native acceptance evidence closure
goal: Close the remaining Pi-native roadmap evidence gaps with executable black-box proof: contract recording, profile-surface fidelity, fake-runner attestation, and ask_operator ask/answer/timeout/restart behavior.
acceptance: Run preflight or equivalent run artifacts record the Pi contract/profile evidence needed to prove which Pi CLI/event surface and worker profile the daemon used.
The effective worker profile summary is identical across run_started/status, final report, handoff JSON, implementation summary, and economics/attestation surfaces.
Fake-runner executions are unmistakably labeled as deterministic test-double evidence in status/report/handoff/economics and cannot be mistaken for real Pi worker implementation evidence.

A black-box ask_operator workflow test proves worker asks a question, daemon persists pending state, status/watch/monitor show the exact answer command, operator answers through daemon state, and the worker continues to completion.
Timeout/unavailable ask_operator behavior falls back to blocked ask-user evidence with durable artifacts instead of losing the question or inventing intent.

Daemon restart/interruption while a question is pending is covered: the question remains inspectable and answering either applies on resume or is rejected with clear resume guidance; it is never silently lost.
PI-02, PI-03, and PI-04 roadmap rows are updated only to states backed by the new executable evidence; any remaining gap is explicitly deferred with a revisit condition.
must_ask_if: The implementation would add a second real worker harness, fallback model failover, or provider auto-login/credential mutation.
The implementation would satisfy ask_operator proof through white-box unit tests only, without a black-box daemon/worker workflow case.
The implementation would make Herdr or the Pi UI extension required for answering blockers or headless operation.
The implementation would mark roadmap rows done without slice/run evidence and declared tests.
